title: Molinia maxima
taxonomy: Moliniopsis is the synonym of the genus of grasses to which Molinia maxima belongs. However,  Molinia maxima  is classified in the family Poaceae. More specifically, it is placed in the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Molinieae. Within this tribe,  Molinia maxima  is the only genus. It is sometimes also classified as a species of the genus Molinia within the family Poaceae.
uses: Molinia maxima, also known as giant puccoon, is native to North America and has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its roots and rhizomes have been used to make a tea that is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is also said to have been used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In addition, Molinia maxima is thought to have been used as a treatment for various digestive issues and to promote wound healing. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Molinia maxima's medicinal uses.
